summary: Matched additional early functions in src/melee/pl/player.c while leaving parts of the file under NON_MATCHING because the whole file could not yet compile and match together. The PR removed a large block of player assembly from asm/melee/pl/player.s, added C implementations/wrappers for player slot/entity operations, expanded StaticPlayer from an opaque byte buffer into a partially documented struct, and introduced S8Vec/S8VecPtr in Dolphin matrix types so lbl_803BCDE0 could be typed more accurately. Review focused on keeping symbol renames stable outside the PR, using decimal assertion line numbers, spacing, and correcting an erroneous unused parameter in a Player_800319C4 prototype.
what_changed: Deleted roughly 312 lines of hand-written/player assembly from asm/melee/pl/player.s for functions in the 0x80031DC8-0x80032218 range, leaving func_8003221C and later assembly in place.

Added C implementations in src/melee/pl/player.c for several player functions, but guarded them with #ifdef NON_MATCHING where the PR author said the functions matched on decomp.me but the whole file could not compile and match yet.
Changed lbl_803BCDE0 from an untyped u32 array declaration to S8Vec lbl_803BCDE0[], requiring a new S8Vec type in include/dolphin/mtx/mtxtypes.h.
Reworked Player_CheckSlot to take only the slot, check slot < 0 or 6 <= slot, and assert with decimal line 102 after review feedback.

Expanded StaticPlayer in src/melee/pl/player.h from u8 data[0xE90] into a partially understood 0xE90-sized struct with named fields for player state, character, slot_type, transformed indices, positions, costume/controller/player IDs, entity pointers, flags, stale move entries, and a trailing buffer.

Added or adjusted prototypes around Player_GetPtrForSlot, Player_80031790, Player_80031848, Player_80031900, Player_800319C4, and external helper functions such as func_800D4F24, func_80390228, func_8008701C, func_800873CC, func_802F6E1C, and func_800D4FF4.

Introduced helper logic that iterates over two transformed player slots and calls entity functions only when the selected player_entity entry is non-null, matching Zelda/Sheik-style transformed-state behavior inferred in comments.
smart_moves: Used NON_MATCHING guards to preserve buildability while keeping decomp.me-matching C available for future cleanup.
Recovered enough of StaticPlayer's layout to replace raw offset arithmetic like 0x0C transformed indices and 0xB0 entity pointers with meaningful field access.
Typed a three-byte signed vector table as S8Vec rather than leaving it as u32[], improving data-access expression fidelity.
Centralized repeated slot validation in Player_CheckSlot, mirroring repeated assembly patterns that OSReport then __assert on invalid slots.
Modeled transformed player handling with transformed[2] and player_entity[2], making repeated loops over normal/transformed entities easier to express in C.
decomp_lessons: When a function matches in isolation but not in-file, preserve progress under NON_MATCHING rather than forcing risky global changes.
Struct expansion is useful for matching player code because offsets such as 0x0C, 0xAC, and 0xB0 recur across functions and become clearer as transformed indices, flags, and entity pointers.
Assertion line numbers should match source conventions and reviewer preference; in this PR, hex 0x66 was requested to be written as decimal 102.
Do not add phantom unused parameters to prototypes to satisfy guesses; reviewer noted Player_800319C4 is called by 80031FB0 with only r3 and r4.

Avoid opportunistic renames in unrelated assembly callsites during matching PRs unless the rename is accepted; review requested reverting a grshrineroute call label from func_8008688C_all_players back to Player_80031900.
Small formatting nits, such as adding a blank line between functions, were part of review expectations even in matching work.
naming_conventions: Existing player functions retained mixed placeholder/address-style names such as Player_80031790, Player_80031848, Player_80031900, Player_800319C4, and Player_GetPtrForSlot.
Reviewer feedback favored preserving the existing public callsite name Player_80031900 over a more descriptive but unapproved rename func_8008688C_all_players.

New struct fields used descriptive snake_case where meaning was inferred, including player_state, player_character, slot_type, transformed, nametag_pos, player_entity, flags, and stale_move_table_current_write_index.
Unknown or uncertain fields stayed in unk-offset style such as unk0E, unk34, unk45, unk8F, unkEC, preserving uncertainty rather than overnaming.
Dolphin vector typedefs follow existing capitalization and pointer alias conventions: S16Vec/S16VecPtr and newly added S8Vec/S8VecPtr.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched repeated slot bounds checks as slot < 0 || 6 <= slot followed by OSReport and __assert.
Modeled array indexing into lbl_80453080 through StaticPlayer* and lbl_80453080[slot] instead of manual multiplication by 0xE90.
Translated transformed entity selection from byte-indexed loads at offsets 0x0C/0x0D and pointer loads at 0xB0 into player->player_entity[player->transformed[i]].
Used small inline helper/wrapper logic for repeated calls across both transformed entity slots.
Kept unmatched or file-sensitive code paths behind #else asm blocks to avoid regressing matching while moving C forward.

review_feedback.comment: Reviewer PsiLupan asked to change a call label back, making this the primary nitpick; the hunk showed Player_80031900 being renamed to func_8008688C_all_players.
Reviewer requested changing assertion line number 0x66 to the actual decimal number.
Reviewer requested adding a blank line between adjacent functions around the NON_MATCHING wrapper area.
Reviewer noted there is not an unused argument in Player_800319C4; it is called by 80031FB0 and only receives r3 and r4.
PR author said requested changes were completed and invited maintainers to squash commits on merge.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove NON_MATCHING around Player_80031790, Player_80031848, Player_80031900, or the 0x80031DC8-0x80032218 player.c range.
Search for later refinements to StaticPlayer field names and size validation after this initial struct expansion.
Search for Player_800319C4 prototype changes to confirm the final accepted two-argument signature.
Search for whether S8Vec in include/dolphin/mtx/mtxtypes.h was reused for other signed-byte vector tables.
Search for later rename decisions around Player_80031900 versus func_8008688C_all_players.
